project.name: githubarchive-plus
project.description: An append-only metadata intelligence layer for GitHub repositories that ingests from GH Archive and GitHub Search, stores lightweight repo records, and refreshes richer GitHub details on demand. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to learn what GithubArchive+ is: an append-only metadata intelligence layer that ingests from GH Archive and GitHub Search, stores lightweight repo records in SQLite, enriches on demand, and exposes a SvelteKit UI plus /admin control center. This document sets the mental model for the rest of the tour—ingest first, enrich later, browse and operate through the web app.

tour.title: Architectural Laws
tour.description: ARCHITECTURE_PHILOSOPHY.md states the platform rules that shape every module ahead: evidence before intelligence, immutable append-only history, and metadata-first ingest. Read this before the code so you recognize why the schema, workers, and UI prefer provenance and backlog state over aggressive mutation.
tour.languageLesson: Architecture docs are non-code tour stops that encode product constraints; treat them as contracts the implementation must satisfy, not optional commentary.

tour.title: Runtime Entry Points
tour.description: The app boots through two complementary entry points. src/hooks.server.ts is the SvelteKit server hook that opens the DB, starts background work, sets admin locals, and guards /admin. scripts/start-production.ts is the production launcher that spawns the web build alongside the full scripts/daemon.ts pipeline tree—useful when you need the whole ops stack, not just request handling.

tour.title: SQLite Data Foundation
tour.description: All pipeline and UI state lands in SQLite. schema.ts owns the authoritative migrations; connection.ts provides the WAL-mode better-sqlite3 singleton; migrate.ts and ready.ts apply schema and gate routes until the DB is safe to use. This layer is the shared substrate for ingest hours, enrichment queues, daemon decisions, and admin stats.
tour.languageLesson: better-sqlite3 is a synchronous SQLite driver: a single shared connection with WAL mode keeps reads concurrent while writes stay serialized—ideal for a local intelligence store.

tour.title: Ingest Pipeline
tour.description: Ingest turns public GitHub activity into append-only repo records. gharchive.ts parses GH Archive hour streams for Repository CreateEvents; scripts/lib/ingest-core.ts orchestrates hour ingest with retries and Search fallback; workers/ingest.ts fills missing hours in daemon cycles; repo-discovery.ts shards GitHub Search windows when Archive coverage is thin. Together they implement the README’s “GH Archive + Search → SQLite” path.

tour.title: Daemon Planner Loop
tour.description: Building on ingest workers, the background daemon decides what to run next. background-daemon.ts is the long-running loop; daemon-backlog.ts measures enrichment, archive, search-gap, and backfill pressure; daemon-planner.ts scores the next action and sleep interval from backlog plus rate limits; daemon_decisions persists those choices for ops visibility. This is the autonomous planner that keeps the pipeline moving without constant CLI intervention.

tour.title: Enrichment Pipeline
tour.description: After lightweight ingest, enrichment deepens repository intelligence on demand. enrich.ts syncs GitHub metadata, metrics, releases, and rename history; enrichment-queue.ts claims work with tiered priority and retries; workers/enrich.ts runs burst-capable claim batches with stage timings. The daemon from Step 6 feeds this queue whenever enrichment backlog dominates other work.

tour.title: Jobs and Pipeline Workers
tour.description: job-runner.ts serializes ingest, enrich, archive, backup, backfill, and export runs with job bookkeeping, while run-pipeline.ts offers a one-shot CLI that walks the full discovery stages. Specialist workers—archive, refresh, classify, cluster, score, emerging—extend the same pattern so admin APIs and the daemon can queue named actions consistently.

tour.title: Discovery UI
tour.description: The SvelteKit presentation layer turns SQLite intelligence into browseable discovery. The homepage loaders assemble search filters, discovery lanes, and signal counts; discover routes surface presets like emerging topics and deleted gems; shared components such as DiscoveryRepoCard and DiscoverySections keep the feed dense and navigable. This is where enriched, clustered repos become a product surface.
tour.languageLesson: SvelteKit +page.server.ts files run on the server to load data; the matching +page.svelte consumes that data for rendering—keeping DB access off the client.

tour.title: Admin Control Center
tour.description: The /admin area is the ops cockpit promised in the README. admin.ts aggregates daemon UI state, jobs, backfill, rate limits, and archive metrics; the admin dashboard page exposes start/stop controls and health views; auth.ts protects those surfaces with password sessions. From here operators steer the ingest and enrichment machinery you just walked through.

tour.title: Foundry Orchestrator
tour.description: tools/foundry is a nested secondary subsystem—a local AI software-engineering orchestrator, not part of the GH Archive ingest path. Its README and index.ts/server.ts/relay.ts loop show how GPT planning drives Cursor agents with approvals and verification. Treat Foundry as a companion toolchain that can operate on this repo, separate from the metadata intelligence platform.

tour.title: Config and Deployment
tour.description: Close the tour with how the platform is configured and shipped. package.json defines Vite/SvelteKit and CLI worker scripts; .env.example documents GitHub auth, SQLite paths, and metadata-first knobs; the Dockerfile builds a Node 20 production image; railway.toml deploys that image with a health check on '/'. These glue the entry points, workers, and UI into a runnable service.
tour.languageLesson: Railway’s railway.toml can point build at a Dockerfile and restart on health-check failure—pairing container packaging with platform-level process supervision.
